metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 6 B
metadata.notes: Public-web research only. Historical breeder, employer, applicant, assignee, licensee, successor, and trademark roles remain distinct; no external communication was made. Supplied canonicalName strings are preserved even where a record has reversed name order.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - independent private breeder with multiple commercial rightsholders
profiles.ownershipSummary: Independent raspberry breeder associated with Faver, Trentino. Rights are cultivar-specific: Sant'Orsola entities appear on Vajolet Plus and Lagorai Plus records, while Molari & Gatti or related Molari entities appear on Enrosadira and other records; no source supports rolling his entire historical portfolio into one company.
profiles.headquarters: Breeder activity associated with Faver, Trentino, Italy; Enrosadira commercialization contact in Cesena, Emilia-Romagna, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: No named personal successor was found. Molari's family management provides commercialization continuity for its records, but that does not establish ownership of Telch cultivars held by Sant'Orsola or by Telch himself.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Verified as one individual, not a company or duplicate. Preserve the applicant, assignee, and holder shown for each cultivar and territory.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - former public-institution breeder and research director
profiles.ownershipSummary: Hideo Bessho was a NARO fruit-breeding researcher and division director. NARO and its Ministry of Agriculture predecessor entities, not Bessho personally, are the documented institutional owners of the associated apple and grape work.
profiles.headquarters: NARO headquarters, 3-1-1 Kannondai, Tsukuba, Ibaraki, Japan; apple breeding historically centered at the Morioka fruit-tree station
profiles.leadershipSummary: He was Director of NARO's Division of Fruit Breeding and Genetics in 2018 and is now publicly described as former NARO. Program continuity is institutional and team-based; no evidence supports personal ownership or a private successor vehicle.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Name order follows the supplied source string. Treat this as Hideo Bessho and retain the public institution named on each historical filing.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Legacy apple breeder from the Dresden-Pillnitz public breeding program, now represented by the Julius Kuhn Institute (JKI) and its federal predecessors. The cultivar rights are institutional public-sector assets, not personal property.
profiles.headquarters: JKI Institute for Breeding Research on Fruit Crops, Pillnitzer Platz 3a, 01326 Dresden-Pillnitz, Germany
profiles.leadershipSummary: Completed institutional succession. Crista Fischer is absent from the current staff roster; Henryk Flachowsky is institute head and Andreas Peil is a current apple-breeding scientist.
profiles.primaryContactName: JKI Institute for Breeding Research on Fruit Crops
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat East German, later federal, and JKI naming as institutional succession. Do not assign Fischer's historical cultivars to current staff personally.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - private family-company founder and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Australian citrus breeder and family-business founder. Historical title is split among Craig Pressler personally, Craig and Bindi Pressler as trustees for the C&B Pressler Family Trust, and 2PH entities. Costa acquired the 2PH operating business and assets in 2021, but public evidence does not support reassigning every earlier Pressler or trust-held right to Costa.
profiles.headquarters: Superior Production Company and the Pressler breeding activity, Emerald, Queensland 4720, Australia
profiles.leadershipSummary: Craig Pressler and family remain publicly associated with Superior Production Company; Andrew Miles joined as Research Director in 2018. This is evidence of an active post-2PH breeding platform rather than a completed handoff to Costa.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Independent family-company target. Verify title and any 2021 asset transfer cultivar by cultivar; do not treat the sale of 2PH operations as a blanket patent assignment.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - retired public extension breeder with separate private breeding records
profiles.ownershipSummary: Former UC Cooperative Extension farm advisor who co-developed UC pistachios while also appearing on unrelated private table-grape records. The Regents of the University of California own the UC pistachio cultivars; private grape rights were assigned record-by-record to parties including Anton Caratan & Son and, in later foreign records, Luribay Business Inc.
profiles.headquarters: UC Cooperative Extension Kern County, Bakersfield, California, and UC Davis, California; private grape work associated with the Delano area
profiles.leadershipSummary: Maranto is retired or emeritus. Craig Kallsen continued the Kern County pistachio evaluation and extension role, giving the UC program visible institutional succession; that succession has no bearing on Maranto's separate private grape assignments.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not combine the public UC pistachio portfolio with the privately assigned grape portfolio. Historical employer and private assignee must be retained per record.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Yuji Ito is a NARO researcher whose documented career spans apple, citrus, blueberry, and pear programs. Official Japanese variety records name NARO as titleholder, so the mixed-crop portfolio reflects transfers within the public research system rather than duplicate identities or personal ownership.
profiles.headquarters: NARO headquarters, Tsukuba, Ibaraki, Japan; last documented institutional base at NARO Hokkaido Agricultural Research Center, Sapporo, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: A 2025 NARO award notice identifies Ito as retired. Succession is institutional and team-based across NARO centers; no private successor or personal rights vehicle was found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Name order follows the supplied source string. Treat this as Yuji Ito and retain NARO or the named predecessor public body on each historical record.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - independent grower-breeder and personal rightsholder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Kazuo Yoshiie is an independent apple grower-breeder associated with Imashirushi Yoshiie Orchard in Nakano, Nagano. Official variety records name him as breeder and holder for cultivars including Moon Rouge and Kirameki, supporting personal rather than institutional title for those records.
profiles.leadershipSummary: A 2026 Nagano Prefecture release still identifies Yoshiie and his orchard with active cultivar commercialization. No named family or management successor was found, so the portfolio has a visible individual key-person and succession signal.

webResearchSources.label: Official Swiss Kirameki breeder and owner record

profiles.webResearchNotes: Verified as Kazuo Yoshiie despite occasional foreign-record transliteration errors. No official public business contact path was located, so no personal address, email, or phone is included.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - former federal research technician and breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Former USDA Agricultural Research Service grape research technician and co-breeder in the David Ramming program at Parlier. Patents identify the United States, represented by the Secretary of Agriculture, as assignee; the portfolio is federal institutional IP, not Tarailo's personal property.
profiles.headquarters: USDA-ARS San Joaquin Valley Agricultural Sciences Center, 9611 S. Riverbend Avenue, Parlier, California 93648
profiles.leadershipSummary: The Ramming-Tarailo generation is a completed federal-program handoff. Current continuity sits with USDA-ARS and the Parlier center; no personal or family successor vehicle was found.
profiles.primaryContactName: USDA-ARS San Joaquin Valley Agricultural Sciences Center

webResearchSources.label: Autumn King patent and federal assignee

profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat Tarailo as an individual employee-breeder within USDA-ARS. Preserve the United States or Secretary of Agriculture as holder on the historical records.

profiles.ownershipType: individual - research-institute breeder with later private assignees
profiles.ownershipSummary: The cultivar and assignment trail resolves this shortened record to Mark Joseph Stephens of New Zealand's berry-breeding program. Older work was developed through HortResearch or Plant & Food Research, while later NN-series rights were assigned on specific records to Pacific Berries LLC; the two portfolios should not be collapsed into either owner.
profiles.headquarters: Historical berry program at Mt Albert, Auckland, and Motueka, New Zealand; Plant & Food Research's current legal successor is the New Zealand Institute for Bioeconomy Science
profiles.leadershipSummary: Institutional succession is explicit: Plant & Food Research became a division of the New Zealand Institute for Bioeconomy Science on July 1, 2025. Pacific Berries remains a separate private holder on assigned NN-series records; Stephens's current employment was not confirmed.
profiles.primaryContactName: New Zealand Institute for Bioeconomy Science
profiles.webResearchNotes: Not a duplicate, but the supplied name omits the first name Mark used in newer records. Keep historical institution-owned cultivars separate from Pacific Berries assignments and verify each territory independently.

profiles.ownershipType: malformed name-order record - individual corporate breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Malformed reversal of Antonio A. Alamo Bermudo, a blueberry breeder on Fall Creek filings. Most cited FCE and FCM applications are assigned to Fall Creek Farm and Nursery, while ZF06-051 is documented under The Conrad-Pyle Company; neither pattern supports personal title or a blanket single-company rollup.
profiles.headquarters: Fall Creek corporate headquarters in Lowell, Oregon, USA; breeder activity and the EMEA operation in the Seville area, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: Recent 2025 filings continue to list Antonio A. Alamo Bermudo with Fall Creek breeder Peter Boches, showing an active team rather than a completed succession. Fall Creek's global private breeding platform provides institutional continuity.

webResearchSources.label: Fall Creek official locations and contacts

profiles.webResearchNotes: Preserve the supplied canonicalName but normalize identity matching to Antonio A. Alamo Bermudo. Do not merge the Conrad-Pyle record into Fall Creek ownership.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Strawberry breeder working in Driscoll's corporate program on the cited records. PBR and patent records identify Driscoll's, Inc. or predecessor Driscoll Strawberry Associates as holder or assignee; no personal ownership evidence was found.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The relevant releases are products of a multi-breeder corporate program with continuing institutional ownership. Robertson's exact current role was not confirmed, but no sole-person succession dependency or personal rights vehicle appears in the records.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll the cited records into the Driscoll's corporate portfolio while preserving the legal entity named at each filing date. Do not create a personal Robertson ownership record.

profiles.ownershipType: malformed name-order record - deceased public-institution breeder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Malformed reversal of Aliza Vardi (1935-2014), a Volcani Center citriculture breeder. Patents assign her releases to the State of Israel through the Ministry of Agriculture and Agricultural Research Organization, not to Vardi personally.
profiles.headquarters: Volcani Institute, Agricultural Research Organization, 68 HaMakabim Street, Rishon LeZion, Israel; historical breeding work at Bet Dagan, Israel
profiles.leadershipSummary: Vardi died in 2014, so succession is complete and institutional. Current Volcani leadership is headed by Director Benny Chefetz; later program scientists should not be treated as owners of Vardi's historical releases.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Preserve the supplied canonicalName but normalize identity matching to Aliza Vardi. Keep the State of Israel or the named ARO/Ministry entity as historical rightsholder.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Sweet-cherry and plum breeder in the University of Bologna program. University cultivar and patent pages name Alma Mater Studiorum - University of Bologna as the owner or applicant, with Michelangelo Grandi as a breeder or inventor rather than personal rightsholder.
profiles.headquarters: University of Bologna, Department of Agricultural and Food Sciences, Viale Giuseppe Fanin 44-46, Bologna, Italy
profiles.leadershipSummary: The Sweet series is documented as team work with Stefano Lugli and Riccardo Correale, providing institutional bench continuity. Grandi's exact current position was not confirmed, but no personal ownership or private successor vehicle was found.
profiles.primaryContactName: University of Bologna plant-variety rights team
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat as a University of Bologna breeder profile. Preserve the university and any territory-specific licensee or holder; do not assign the portfolio to Grandi personally.
